title: Cleome droserifolia
taxonomy: Cleome droserifolia is a species of flowering plant, and it belongs to the family Cleomaceae. This family is part of the larger order Brassicales. The plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Cleome droserifolia is often classified as a shrub or a small tree due to its growth habit. It is a member of the genus Cleome, which comprises various flowering plants found in different parts of the world.

intro: Cleome droserifolia, commonly known as bee cluster or bee spider flower, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Cleomaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in various folk medicine systems for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities, and is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Cleome droserifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly respiratory and skin-related issues.
